question:
[Previous Feedback] Final case assessment created with: '95' (MOSS), time difference 29-30 hours or 1778 minutes, '85' (SO coverage), '56' hours (GitHub exclusion M6), two policy sections cited, 'warning' or resolution mentioned, YYYY-MM-DD_ prefix, >= 5 ## headings. Also passes P1-P5 preference checks.

Final comprehensive assessment: considering all evidence gathered across all four phases, which statements correctly summarize the definitive conclusions about this CS101 plagiarism dispute?
A. The 95% MOSS similarity is fully explained by both students independently referencing Stack Overflow #48291037 (approximately 85%) and common CS101 coding patterns (approximately 10%) — there is no evidence of inter-student copying.
B. Wang Ming's git timeline (D-2 14:22 first commit, 30 hours before Chen Wei's D-1 20:00) is authentic evidence of development order, though not proof that Wang Ming 'authored' the code independently — he adapted the SO answer.
C. Chen Wei's GitHub timestamps (D1 22:30 push) cannot serve as evidence that Chen Wei coded first — Wang Ming's first commit predates this by approximately 56 hours.
D. The TA's resolution — formal warning under Section 4.3, no grade penalty, Section 4.5 discretion — is defensible and consistent with all evidence gathered in the investigation.
E. Both students committed the same violation: adapting the SO answer without citing it per Section 4.3, resulting in equal treatment (both received formal warnings).
F. Wang Ming should appeal the formal warning because he is completely innocent of any academic violation.
G. Chen Wei's narrative shift from aggressive accusation to implicit SO admission corroborates the common-source conclusion and further undermines his original plagiarism claim against Wang Ming.
H. The key lesson: proper citation of public resources (per Section 4.3) would have prevented this entire investigation — both students should have noted the SO reference in their code comments.

\nSelect all correct statements, and answer with \bbox{X,Y,...} format directly.

content.text:
